table_semantics.justification: Full table structure: <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, <th scope='col'> for plan names, <th scope='row'> for feature names. No layout abuse.

element_choice.justification:
CTAs are elements linking to appropriate pages; prices are

, not headings; plan cards use

; 'Most Popular' is a . Every element is the best semantic match.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1>, two <h2> subsections ('Available Plans', 'Feature Comparison'), <h3> plan names — no skipped levels. Prices and badges are not headings.

aria_discipline.justification: <nav aria-label='Primary'>, <section aria-labelledby>, and disambiguating aria-label on duplicate CTA links are all appropriate. No redundant roles. aria-hidden on Yes/No spans paired with visually-hidden alternatives is deliberate and valid.

landmark_structure.justification: <header>, <nav aria-label>, <main tabindex='-1'>, labelled <section>s, <footer>, and a skip link are all present and correctly scoped.

list_semantics.justification: Plan cards are wrapped in <ul><li><article>, nav items in <ul> inside <nav>. Correct list types throughout.

content_realism.justification: Real plan names, prices, and feature values used. No placeholder text or lorem ipsum.

expectations_met: Feature comparison uses a <table> for two-dimensional data
Plan names are <th scope='col'> in the header row
Feature names are <th scope='row'> in each row
Yes/No values conveyed as visible text; screen readers get 'Included'/'Not included' via visually-hidden spans
'Most Popular' badge is readable text inside the <h3> and therefore accessible to screen readers
CTA links are <a> elements pointing to appropriate pages
'Contact Sales' is a link (<a href='/contact-sales'>), correct since it navigates to a contact page
Plan names above the table use <h3> (appropriate level under <h2> section)
Boolean feature cells use text ('Yes'/'No') — no icon-only approach
expectations_missed: 'Most Popular' in the table header column is aria-hidden='true', so screen reader users navigating the table won't hear it — only users who encounter the plan card <h3> first will. Minor gap in discoverability within the table context.
additional_observations: tabindex='-1' on <main> is a correct pattern enabling the skip link to programmatically focus the landmark
Disambiguating aria-label on duplicate 'Start Free Trial' links (e.g., 'Start Free Trial — Starter plan') is exemplary accessibility practice
